text.title: Agent Replay Harness Cookbook Procedure
text.summary: Seven-step procedure from "we have a failing trace" to "the failure replays on demand".

step.name: capture-baseline-trace
step.input: Live agent run with structured logging enabled.
step.action: Record every LLM call (prompt + completion + finish_reason + temperature + seed), every tool call (name + args + return + duration), and every clock/random read into a jsonl trace keyed by run_id.

step.name: classify-nondeterminism
step.action: Label each event as: stubbable_llm, stubbable_tool, infra_clock, infra_random, infra_external (non-stubbable). Compute coverage = stubbable_events / total_events.
step.decision-gate: If coverage < 0.9, stop and capture more state before continuing.

step.name: wire-llm-stub
step.action: Subclass the agent's LLM client. Override the call method to look up the event by prompt-hash + iteration number and return the recorded completion. On miss, raise ReplayMissError with the prompt diff.
step.output: replay_llm.py + a unit test that replays one recorded turn.

step.name: wire-tool-stubs
step.input: Trace events of class stubbable_tool; tool registry from prerequisites.
step.action: For each tool, generate a stub class that overrides execute() to look up by (tool_name, args_hash) and return the recorded value. Wrap in a typed dataclass matching the tool's schema.
step.output: One stub class per tool + a registry mapping name → stub.

step.name: freeze-clock-and-random
step.input: Trace events of class infra_clock and infra_random.
step.action: Inject a frozen clock (e.g. freezegun) and a seeded random (numpy seed) at agent-init time, restored from trace metadata.
step.output: conftest.py / harness/init.py block setting time + seed.

step.name: run-replay-and-assert-equality
step.input: The wired harness + the original failing trace.
step.action: Execute the agent under the harness. Assert that every emitted event matches the recorded event (LLM completion text, tool args). Diff any divergence.
step.output: Either green (failure reproduces) or a diff report showing the first divergent event.
step.decision-gate: If diff is non-empty, root-cause it (usually missing state — go back to step 2 with the new event class) before declaring the harness done.

step.name: publish-manifest-and-deviation-log
step.action: Emit the harness manifest per content/02-output-contract.xml. Open a deviation-log entry for any tool that needed a non-trivial stub (e.g. partial response, schema mismatch).
step.output: Validated harness-manifest.json + deviation-log entry committed to the repo.
